name: busquedas_internas_agent
description: Searches Accenture's internal ATS (Workday) for candidates matching a structured Job Description. Reads each candidate's CV, extracts a structured profile, and returns a ranked list with AI-generated compatibility scores, skill gap analysis, and justifications.
when_to_use: When the user wants to search for internal candidates, rank current employees against a job description, or find the best internal profiles that match a given role. Always use this AFTER the job_description_agent has structured the JD.

actions.name: buscar_candidatos
actions.description: Queries the ATS for internal candidates matching the provided structured Job Description. For each candidate found, extracts a structured profile from the CV and computes a compatibility score (0.0–1.0) weighted by skill match (70%), management level (15%), and experience (15%). Returns up to all found candidates sorted by score descending.

request_schema.role_title: string — job title exactly as it appears in the JD
request_schema.management_level: Individual Contributor | Team Lead | Manager | Senior Manager | Director | Vice President

conventions.management_levels: Must be exactly one of: 'Individual Contributor', 'Team Lead', 'Manager', 'Senior Manager', 'Director', 'Vice President'. When ambiguous, always choose the lower level.
conventions.skills_format: PascalCase for technologies and tools (e.g., 'Python', 'AWS', 'PostgreSQL', 'FastAPI'); lowercase in Spanish for soft skills (e.g., 'liderazgo', 'comunicación efectiva'). Max 15 skills.
conventions.candidato_rankeado_fields: Each ranked candidate includes: nombre, apellido, email, cargo_actual, departamento, skills, experiencia_años, nivel_management, pais, disponibilidad, score (0.0–1.0), justificacion, habilidades_match, habilidades_faltantes.
conventions.state: Stateless: each request is independent. The agent does not persist ranking results — the orchestrator must store whatever it needs for downstream agents.
